subject: Project Management
title: Lean vs Agile
description: A short lesson to help you apply Lean vs Agile.

hook.text: Lean eliminates waste. Agile embraces change. Are they competitors or cousins?

buildup.text: Both value small batches, rapid feedback, and respect for people. Both oppose heavy upfront planning.

discovery.text: Lean focuses on flow efficiency. Agile focuses on adaptability. They solve different parts of the same puzzle.

twist.text: The Agile Manifesto was heavily influenced by lean thinking — Mary and Tom Poppendieck made the link explicit.

climax.text: Smart teams don't choose between lean and Agile. They use both — lean for flow, Agile for flexibility.

punchline.text: Different names. Same family. Complementary superpowers.

quiz.question: How do lean and Agile relate to each other?
options: They are complementary — lean focuses on flow, Agile on adaptability
